published.text:
### Hero
Heading: n8n vs. Make for Small Business
Body: Make (formerly Integromat) and n8n are both powerful automation platforms that go well beyond what Zapier offers. But they differ on pricing, self-hosting, AI integration, and how they handle complex logic. This guide compares them specifically for small business automation — not enterprise, not developer hobby projects.
Sub: Below: pricing models, feature comparison, complexity handling, and guidance on which fits your situation.

### Core sections
- Two powerful automation platforms (cards: n8n, Make, When either works)
- Side-by-side comparison (table: pricing, self-hosting, AI integration, complexity, learning curve, integrations)
- When Make wins vs when n8n wins (when-to-use)
- What actually matters beyond features (considerations: cost at scale, vendor lock-in, community, AI-native)
- Case studies referencing existing projects

### FAQ
Subhead: Practical answers about n8n vs. Make for small businesses

### Final CTA
Heading: Need help choosing the right platform?
Body: Book a 30-minute call. We will look at your automation needs and figure out which platform makes the most sense.

edits.description: Published as part of batch 4. Extends the automation-tool comparison cluster alongside n8n-vs-zapier.
Interlinks to n8n vs Zapier, n8n automation examples, GoHighLevel vs custom CRM.
Positions Make as strong for visual users; n8n as the power choice for scale and AI.
References self-hosting cost advantages with real numbers.
